title: Why are some constellations visible for only part of the year?
summary: Why do some constellations stay in the sky all year, while others disappear?

title: Science history: Chernobyl nuclear power plant melts down, bringing the world to the brink of disaster — April 26, 1986
summary: On April 26, 1986, the Chernobyl nuclear reactor melted down, but the rest of the world wouldn't learn how close it came to nuclear Armageddon until weeks later.

title: تحولات
summary: تقدم نتائج البحث الوطني حول العائلة أرضية خصبة لقراءة تتجاوز الوصف الإحصائي نحو تحليل ديناميات التحول البنيوي داخل المجتمع المغربي، فالعائلة، باعتبارها وحدة سوسيولوجية مركزية، لم تعد مجرد إطار للتنشئة الاجتماعية، بل أصبحت فضاء تتقاطع داخله رهانات الاقتصاد، والتحولات الثقافية، وإعادة توزيع الأدوار ب…
body: تقدم نتائج البحث الوطني حول العائلة أرضية خصبة لقراءة تتجاوز الوصف الإحصائي نحو تحليل ديناميات التحول البنيوي داخل المجتمع المغربي، فالعائلة، باعتبارها وحدة سوسيولوجية مركزية، لم تعد مجرد إطار للتنشئة الاجتماعية، بل أصبحت فضاء تتقاطع داخله رهانات الاقتصاد، والتحولات الثقافية، وإعادة توزيع الأدوار بين الأفراد. وأحد أبرز أكمل القراءة » المقالة تحولات fمنشورة على جريدة الصباح .

title: Thríhnúkagígur: The only volcano on Earth where you can descend into a magma chamber
summary: Thríhnúkagígur is a volcano near Reykjavík in Iceland with an empty magma chamber decorated with vivid colors that scientists and tourists can access via an open cable elevator.

title: New York City is at major risk of flooding that could leave 4.4 million people exposed to extreme damage, study finds
summary: The new flood risk index identified eight cities along the U.S. East Coast that are at high or very high risk of floods causing extreme damage.

title: 'A completely new reality': Bolder measures are needed to prevent extreme water shortages in cities like Phoenix and Las Vegas that depend on the Colorado River
summary: Cities fed by the Colorado River have taken huge steps to reduce their water consumption over the past few decades, yet water shortages are projected to grow more intense. What can be done?

title: Planning to stargaze in May 2026? Here's all the gear you'll need
summary: Here's all the stargazing gear you need for May's night sky. Be ready for two full moons, a meteor shower, and a pretty cool solar alignment.

title: DNA study of nearly 200 Indigenous genomes reveals unknown Asian 'ghost' population contributed to American ancestry
summary: New genetic results reveal a previously unknown wave of people settled in South America 1,300 years ago and that Indigenous Americans carry remnants of a "ghost lineage."

title: اتهام جندي أميركي باستغلال معلومات اعتقال مادورو في مراهنات
summary: يواجه جندي أميركي اتهاما باستخدام معلومات داخلية لربح 400 ألف دولار في سوق مراهنات عبر الإنترنت حول اعتقال الرئيس الفنزويلي نيكولاس مادورو، حسبما أعلن مسؤولون اتحاديون يوم الخميس.

title: 'A measurable, enormous global impact': Astronaut Chris Hadfield on why the true power of Artemis II could take decades to hit
summary: Astronaut Chris Hadfield shares his emotional response to the Artemis II mission, and why it could change the course of people's futures.

title: 'Kraken' octopus that lived at the time of the dinosaurs was a 62-foot-long apex predator of the ocean
summary: A close inspection of 27 fossil jaws from finned octopuses challenge the longstanding belief that the apex oceanic predators of the Cretaceous were all vertebrates.

title: Artemis II heat shield aced its blistering reentry, ghostly underwater photo reveals
summary: The Orion heat shield used for the Artemis II mission held up perfectly, early photos and a NASA assessment reveal.

title: NASA's Curiosity rover finds a surprising number of giant 'dragon scales' littered across Mars
summary: The Curiosity rover snapped a series of peculiar polygons that look suspiciously like giant fossilized reptile scales. Although scientists have seen similar shapes on Mars before, they have never seen such a "dramatically abundant" concentration.

title: Earth quiz: What do you know about our planet's most amazing features?
summary: Do you know Earth's highest peak, deepest point, and more? Test your knowledge about our planet in this quiz.

title: NASA shuts off another Voyager 1 instrument as humanity's most distant spacecraft prepares for risky 'Big Bang' maneuver to save power
summary: After nearly 50 years in space, the two Voyager spacecraft are very low on nuclear power. Voyager 1 just shut off another instrument to save the mission.

title: Neanderthal toddlers grew faster than modern humans, probably because of the harsh environment they evolved in
summary: A new study of a Neanderthal toddler reveals that our closest evolutionary relatives' growth patterns differed from those of modern humans.

title: Glowing ring of plankton surrounding New Zealand islands linked to deadly underwater plateau — Earth from space
summary: A recent satellite photo captured a gleaming halo of phytoplankton encircling the remote Chatham Islands. The stunning sight is the result of a hidden underwater structure, which has also helped kill hundreds of cetaceans.

title: Bruce the parrot is missing his upper beak —‬ but that hasn't stopped him from becoming an undefeated jousting champion
summary: A kea parrot in New Zealand who lost the top part of his beak when young has developed a new way to fight other males that has made him unbeatable.

title: Altar to Sol: A rare 1,900-year-old monument dedicated to the Roman god of light and used in a secret underground ritual
summary: This unique carved altar represents the triumph of light over darkness in ancient Roman religion.

title: Naked mole rats wage bloody wars of succession to choose a new queen — but one colony did something scientists have never seen before
summary: When their queen dies, naked mole rat females usually wage bloody battles of succession. But peace may be possible, a new study suggests

title: Can chickens really run around with their heads cut off?
summary: There's lore about chickens surviving from seconds to months after their heads are chopped off, but what does the science say?

title: فيديو.. هبوط رواد "أرتيميس 2" في مياه المحيط الهادئ
summary: هبطت مركبة مهمة "أرتيميس 2" التي حملت 4 رواد فضاء حول القمر، الجمعة، في مياه المحيط الهادئ مقابل سواحل كاليفورنيا، كما هو مخطط لها.

title: على شكل "كرة نار".. رواد الفضاء يستعدون للعودة إلى الأرض
summary: يستعد رواد الفضاء الذين أرسلتهم وكالة الفضاء الأميركية "ناسا" إلى القمر في إطار مهمة "أرتيمس 2" للعودة إلى الأرض، وذلك بعد انطلاقهم في مطلع أبريل الجاري.
